reviews.review_description: Lack of content makes me bored after 2 minutes. You have what....10 images...that's it?

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback. We realize the current Gear VR content catalog is rather limited. We're definitely working on that (and getting more photographers capturing PanoMoments). There should be an app update in the next few weeks which will open up a bunch more content to the Gear VR app. In the meantime you should be able to download the Google Play Store version and find more content here - https://www.panomoments.com/gallery - Please reach out to Info@PanoMoments.com if you have any issues with that.

reviews.review_description: I really like this form of blending photo with movement. It's much clearer than 360° video and gives more immersion. I really felt like I was there in the scenes. Really excited for the possibilities of this! I recently did a system update to nougat so now the app works with my phone too so o can't wait to check out more scenes. If thinking about downloading this, you won't be disappointed!

developer_response.body: Exactly! Glad you like the concept. It's the motion parallax (and lack of stitching) that adds that extra immersion. Would you be able to provide some more details on the compatibility issue on your Note 5? I believe it should be compatible as long as you're on Android Nougat. Is it visible in the app store, or does it have an error when running? Thanks for your support.

reviews.review_description: Assuming this is the same Pano company that makes the amazing 4k and 8k VR travel vids on YouTube,  some of the best out there. These Moments are a small loop of action where the action moves forward as you turn around. It's a cool concept, but I'm not really sure it adds much vs either a photo or video, and the constant spinning isn't that fun. Interesting concept, not sold yet on the entertainment value

developer_response.body: Hey! Glad you like the concept. We're actually not related to those travel videos on YouTube, but we'd love to reach out to those folks to see if we can convince them to start capturing PanoMoments. Would you be able to share their info to us? Feel free to reach out via Info@PanoMoments.com - Also, be sure to check out some of the additional content available here - https://www.panomoments.com/gallery

reviews.review_description:
360 panos with movement. Quite interesting.

Not a lot of content though.

developer_response.body: Hey Mike, glad you like the concept. We're definitely working on adding more content to the Gear VR app (and getting more photographers capturing PanoMoments). There should be an app update in the next few weeks. In the meantime you should be able to download the Google Play Store version and find more content here - https://www.panomoments.com/gallery - Please reach out to Info@PanoMoments.com if you have any issues with that.
